title: No rate limiting on login attempts
description: The receiveSendLogin function processes login attempts without any rate limiting, delay, or lockout mechanism. An attacker can perform unlimited brute-force login attempts against any account. The function simply checks credentials and returns true/false with no throttling, no failed attempt counter, no IP-based blocking, and no exponential backoff. Combined with the SHA-256 password hashing (no salting, no bcrypt/scrypt/argon2), this makes brute-force attacks highly practical.
recommendation: Implement a rate limiter that tracks failed login attempts per IP address and per username. After N failed attempts (e.g. 5), introduce an exponential backoff delay or temporary account lockout. Consider using a token bucket or sliding window algorithm. Also consider replacing SHA-256 password storage with bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 for password hashing at rest.
